content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Mickey Bennett
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, 2nd Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Date: January 27, 1977

From : Secretary of State - Executive Office

Subject: Magnani Letter

As the attached indicates, we have responded
to Laura Magnani denying her request. This
denial is consistent with our understanding
of the Act which does not appear to except
lobbyists for non-profit entities from the
fee requirement.

Sincerely,

ANTHONY D. MILLER
Chief Counsel

ALM:gp
Attachment

FRIENDS COMMITTEE ON LEGISLATION OF CALIFORNIA
1107 9th STREET • ROOM 207 • SACRAMENTO • CALIFORNIA 95814 • (916) 442-4744

February 24, 1977

William Durley
Elections & Political Reform
Office of Secretary of State
Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Dear William Durley:

Thank you for your letter of January 10, 1977 and the
materials on the Political Reform Act. We have reviewed the
materials with our attorney. She advises us that the Political
Reform Act does not require the assessment of a lobbyist registration fee; the Act provides an exception whereby lobbyists may be
charged "no more than $25" for registration. (Sec. 81006).

Your office is aware, no doubt, that a number of low-budget
public interest lobbies have come to Sacramento in the last few
years. It is well within the discretion of the office of Secretary
of State, and it would further the purposes of the Political Reform
Act, to charge a lower registration fee to such groups. It is
also reasonable and within your discretion to charge a proportionately lower fee for part-time lobbyists.

Access to government officials and representatives is a
fundamental part of the democratic process. I'm sure you agree
that such access should not be taxed, and that any registration
fee levied should be minimal and should reflect only actual
registration costs.

In registering two additional lobbyists (see enclosed forms),
both of whom will lobby on a part-time basis, we request again
that the registration fee be reduced or waived.

Sincerely,

Laura Magnani
Legislative Advocate

LM:gm
cc: David Pitman, Sect. of State
    Michael Burnett, FPPC

January 10, 1977


Laura Magnani
Legislative Advocata
FRIENDS COMMITTEE ON
LEGISLATION OF CALIFORNIA
107 - 9th Street, Room 1037
Sacramento, California    95814

Dear Ms. Magnani:

The Lobbyist Registration Statement submitted by you for
the 1977-78 legislative session was received unsigned.
Please affix your signature and place the date of your
signing on the verification of the attached photocopy and
return it to this office as soon as practical. Your re-
gistration will not be regarded as complete until we re-
ceive the signed statement.

With reference to your questions regarding the registra-
tion fee and reporting requirements, we recommend you
review the enclosed Instruction Manual for Lobbyists and
the Instruction Manual for Form 650 for Persons Spending
$250 or More to Influence Legislative or Administrative
Action and for Employers of Lobbyists. Also for your con-
venience, is a copy of the Political Reform Act of 1974.
This material is provided to enable you to determine the
actions required of you and your organization by the
Political Reform Act.

As you can see, the reporting requirements and maximum
fee for lobbyists are prescribed in the law. The only
flexibility allowed filling officers is the limited dis-
cretion to waive liabilities incurred for late submission
of statements and reports. Each person registering as a
lobbyist is required to pay a $25 fee to this office.

Laura Magnani                                January 10, 1977




If you have further questions pertaining to the above
matter, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform


DAVID B. PITHAN, Manager
Political Reform Division

DBP/cs'
Enclosures




-2-

FRIENDS COMMITTEE ON LEGISLATION
OF CALIFORNIA
1107 9th Street, Room 1031
Sacramento, California 95814


December 21, 1976

March Fong Eu
Secretary of State
925 [illegible]
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear March Fong Eu:

The purpose of this letter is to request that the Friends
Committee on Legislation be given a fee waiver for the $25
lobbyist registration fee. The FCL believes that such a fee
places an undue hardship on non-profit, by budget organizations
and that the fee, if levied, should be on a sliding scale basis.
It is my understanding that you have the authority to set the
fee and to grant waivers.

I am enclosing a check for my own registration as a lobbyist
in order to meet the December 27th deadline but I hope you will refund
the fee to me at the earliest possible convenience. Because the
FCL anticipates registering one or two more lobbyists for the
coming session--who will be working on a part time basis--we are
extremely concerned about the expense of registration--not to
mention monthly triplicate reporting.

Last session we received no response to our request for a
fee waiver, either from your office, or the Fair Political Practices
Commission. This year I hope you will see fit not only to respond,
but to grant the request.

Sincerely,


Laura Magnani
Legislative Advocate

LM:cm
cc: Michael Bennett, FPC
Encl.
